keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Connectivity Hubs platform engineered to dedicated, enterprise-grade physical fiber connections between external core networks and Google Cloud data centers..
Integrates Cloud Interconnect capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Enterprise Networking and Security Architecture portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Establishes globally routing, logically isolated networks spanning multiple Google Cloud regions privately.
Guarantees highly available hybrid connectivity to on-premises sites using redundant VPN tunnels or private fiber paths.
Balances user traffic dynamically at Google Cloud's edge, directing requests to the lowest-latency healthy backend instance.
Optimizes Cloud Interconnect performance through automated platform scaling and resource balancing.
Enables resource segregation, logical naming conventions, and tag-based configuration policies for Cloud Interconnect.
Integrates with VPC routing and private service access architectures to secure communication tunnels.
Governs access to Cloud Interconnect using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/interconnect.admin and roles/interconnect.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Cloud Interconnect that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Cloud Interconnect resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Cloud Interconnect operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Cloud Interconnect resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Cloud Interconnect resource
commands.command:
gcloud compute interconnects create prod-interconnect-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Cloud Interconnect Resource"

commands.description: List active Cloud Interconnect instances and query configuration states
commands.command: gcloud compute interconnects list --format="table(name, state, updateTime)"
